title: Food processing system and associated method
abstract: The invention relates to a food processing system (100) allowing reconstitution and/or structuration and/or texturization of dehydrated food materials such as powders, the system comprising: - a dosing system (50) comprising one or a plurality of containers (20) each container comprising a dehydrated or partially dehydrated food material; - at least one reconstitution food processing unit (10) to which the containers (20) from the dosing system can be connected, the processing unit (10) comprising: at least one product inlet (17) for the food material coming from a container (20), at least one liquid inlet (18) for liquid to be mixed with the food material, and at least one product outlet (16) at one end of the unit (10) for in-line depositing the formed food; - a depositing area (60) where the formed food from the processing unit (10) is dispensed; the depositing area (60) and the at least one food processing unit (10) being moveable with respect to each other in such a way that different food shapes and/or patterns from one or different food materials can be formed in the depositing area (60). The invention further relates to a method for operating a food processing system (100) as the one described.
claims:
Claims Claims 1 . Food processing system (100) allowing reconstitution and/or structuration and/or texturization of dehydrated food materials such as powders, the system comprising: a dosing system (50) comprising one or a plurality of containers (20) each container comprising a dehydrated or partially dehydrated food material; - at least one reconstitution food processing unit (10) to which the containers (20) from the dosing system can be connected, the processing unit (10) comprising: at least one product inlet (17) for the food material coming from a container (20), at least one liquid inlet (18) for liquid to be mixed with the food material, and at least one product outlet (16) at one end of the unit (10) for in-line depositing the formed food; a depositing area (60) where the formed food from the processing unit (10) is dispensed; the depositing area (60) and the at least one food processing unit (10) being moveable with respect to each other in such a way that different food shapes and/or patterns from one or different food materials can be formed in the depositing area (60). 2. Food processing system (100) according to claim 1 wherein the at least one reconstitution food processing unit (10) and/or the depositing area (60) are relatively moveable in axis X and/or Y and/or Z and/or are relatively rotatable in Theta. 3. Food processing system (100) according to any of the previous claims wherein different containers (20) are interchangeable in the reconstitution food processing unit (10) so as to deposit one or a plurality of layers and/or patterns of the same or different food materials in the depositing area (60). 4. Food processing system (100) according to any of the previous claims further comprising cooking means that can be selectively activated in order to heat and/or cook at least part of the food patterns deposited in the depositing area (60). 5. Food processing system (100) according to claim 4 wherein the cooking means are attached to the at least one reconstitution food processing unit (10). 6. Food processing system (100) according to claim 4 wherein the cooking means are independently moveable from the reconstitution food processing unit (10). 7. Food processing system (100) according to any of the previous claims, the processing unit (10) further comprising heating means allowing heating one or a plurality of pre-defined areas in the processing unit (10) to pre-heat the deposited food material. 8. Food processing system (100) according to any of the previous claims wherein the processing unit (10) comprises at least a heating area to influence and/or modify the food material structure. 9. Food processing system according to claim 8 further comprising at least a cooling area arranged after the heating area where the material structure is established and aligned to the shape of the outlet (16), the outlet being configured as a die. 10. Food processing system (100) according to any of the previous claims wherein the processing speed/rate of the processing unit (10) and/or the product flow rate and/or the liquid flow rate in the processing unit (10) and/or the product temperature are independently controllable to generate different hydration levels and/or food material structures and/or textures of the food product deposited. 1 1 . Food processing system (100) according to any of the previous claims wherein the reconstitution food processing unit (10) is an extrusion unit. 12. Food processing system (100) according to claim 1 1 wherein the extrusion unit comprises two parallel screws (12) configuring one or a plurality of segments with different profiles designed to achieve different functions such as shear generation and/or mixing and/or aeration, and to thrust the product flowing through them. 13. Food processing system (100) according to any of claims 1 1 -12 wherein the extrusion unit comprises on one end a segment (120) avoiding backflow of the product. 14. Food processing system (100) according to any of the previous claims wherein the liquid inlet (18) is arranged after the product inlet (17) with respect to the product path in the processing unit (10). 15. Food processing system (100) according to any of the previous claims wherein the liquid introduced through the liquid inlet (18) has been previously heated. 16. Food processing system (100) according to any of the previous claims comprising a secondary liquid inlet (19) close to the outlet (16). 17. Food processing system (100) according to any of the previous claims wherein the containers (20) in the dosing system comprise identification means with information on the ingredient delivered to the processing unit (10). 18. Food processing system (100) according to any of the previous claims further comprising control means managing the container or containers (20) to be connected to the processing unit (10) so as to deliver different food patterns in the shape of layers of the same or different food materials on the depositing area (60) according to a certain pre-defined recipe in a database or in a control unit. 19. Method for operating a food processing system (100) according to any of claims 1 -18, comprising the steps of: attaching one container (20) to the product inlet (17) to deliver a certain dehydrated food material into the processing unit (10); retrieving the information of the food material on the container (20) and the recipe from a database or a control unit to adapt process parameters in the processing unit (10) to deliver the hydration level and/or food material structure and/or texture desired for the food product; - processing the dehydrated material in the unit (10) and delivering at least a layer of it into the depositing area (60). 20. Method according to claim 19 further comprising a step of changing the container (20) to a different one in order to dispense a further layer of the 

 same or of a different food material, this step being repeated one or a plurality of successive times. Method according to any of claims 19-20 wherein the layers of the product dispensed can be selectively heated and/or cooked by cooking means.
